caption.full:
Top Gun heirs take Paramount lawsuit to the Supreme Court

The heirs of Ehud Yonay are appealing a dismissed lawsuit against Paramount to the Supreme Court. The lawsuit claimed Paramount should have renegotiated a deal for Top Gun: Maverick, but a federal judge threw out the case in 2024.

Do you think the heirs have a valid argument, or should the Supreme Court leave the 2024 dismissal in place?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#MovieNews #BoxOffice #TopGunMaverick #Paramount
caption.firstComment: Interesting twist — the heirs are pushing this to the Supreme Court after a 2024 dismissal. Do you see their claim as a legitimate dispute, or is Paramount in the clear?

youtube.title: Supreme Court Hears Top Gun: Maverick Lawsuit
youtube.description:
The Supreme Court will hear the case brought by the heirs of Ehud Yonay against Paramount over Top Gun: Maverick. The lawsuit claims a deal renegotiation was needed, but a 2024 ruling found no material similarity to Yonay's 1983 article. The Ninth Circuit's copyright test is being examined.

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

Watch more: https://thescreenreport.com/movies/top-gun-maverick-lawsuit-appeal-heads-to-supreme-court-heirs-argue/
